output.summary: Corrected the implementation by removing the incorrectly created blocks/Form/Form.css file. The Gravity Forms styles already exist in the global resources/css/screen/forms.css file and are comprehensive and correct. Verified that the build passes and the global forms.css provides all necessary styling for Gravity Forms elements including inputs, selects, textareas, checkboxes, radios, submit buttons, validation states, and dark/mid/light mode variants.

fileDeleted.reason: Duplicate/incorrect creation - Gravity Forms styles already exist globally in resources/css/screen/forms.css
fileDeleted.previousPurpose: Was intended to provide Gravity Forms style overrides, but this was unnecessary duplication

warnings: Webpack performance warnings (size limits) - pre-existing, not related to changes
buildStatus.details: Build completed successfully after removing blocks/Form/Form.css. No errors introduced.

stylingVerification.details: The existing forms.css file provides comprehensive Gravity Forms styling including:
coverage: Base form elements (inputs, textareas, selects, checkboxes, radios)
Gravity Forms CSS custom properties (--gf-ctrl-* variables)
Dark mode variants for form controls
Mid mode variants for form controls
Light mode variants for form controls
Input sizing (min-h-12 for inputs, min-h-32 for textareas)
Select styling with custom dropdown arrows and rounded-full borders
Checkbox/radio styling with rounded-sm/rounded-full and accent colors
Submit button styling with hover/focus states
Special field types (address, name, date, time, phone, website, list, consent, signature, captcha, fileupload, pricing)
Focus states with proper accessibility (focus:border-accent, focus:outline-none)
notes: Forms.css is imported in resources/css/screen.css and compiled into the main screen bundle
Styles are NOT wrapped in @layer to ensure they override Gravity Forms' unlayered default styles
All focus states use the accent color for proper accessibility
Dark/mid/light mode support is comprehensive with proper CSS custom property overrides

correctionSummary.mistake: Created blocks/Form/Form.css when comprehensive Gravity Forms styles already existed globally
correctionSummary.correction: Deleted the duplicate file and confirmed global forms.css provides all necessary styling
correctionSummary.impact: No impact on functionality - the global forms.css was already being loaded and provides identical (and more comprehensive) styling

filesModifiedFromOriginal: blocks/Form/Form.php (still modified - aria-labelledby, gap-y-16, Gravity Forms check, headingId)

testPage.note: Test page created in previous iteration remains valid and functional

notes: The original implementation created a block-specific CSS file (blocks/Form/Form.css) when the theme already has comprehensive global Gravity Forms styling in resources/css/screen/forms.css
This was an error based on the planning spec which stated 'No Form.css exists' - the planning agent did not find the global forms.css file
User feedback correctly identified that the global forms.css provides all necessary Gravity Forms styling
All other changes from the original developer implementation remain valid: aria-labelledby landmark accessibility, gap-y-16 fix, Gravity Forms graceful handling, headingId prop support
